signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Jobs Shock
signals.text: GBP/AUD divergence reaches critical levels as GBP 1W outperformance (+0.623%) meets AUD weakness (-0.821%) following an April jobs shock. The 1.444% G8 spread is driven by a collapse in RBA hike expectations while GBP remains supported by a 60bp carry advantage over USD (neutral) and 435bp over CHF. AUD/USD at 0.7141 faces further downside as the jobs data dims the RBA's hiking bias.

signals.title: EUR/USD — PMI Contraction + Vol Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1632 under pressure as Eurozone Composite PMI falls to a 31-month low, signaling a Q2 GDP contraction. Options markets are pricing significant tail risk with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 5.0%, creating a +2.1% vol risk premium. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% provides a +175bp carry advantage to USD, structurally limiting EUR upside as growth data deteriorates.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol Risk Premium
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3445 shows signs of late-trend exhaustion despite being the G8 leader. COT LF net long is crowded at +40,980 (70% long) while the options market demands a massive +4.0% vol risk premium (IV 11.0% vs HV30 7.0%). 25d RR at -1.10 confirms a moderate put skew as traders hedge against a reversal following the UK Services PMI slide to 47.9.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.98 remains supported by a +278bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.51% vs TONA 0.73%). However, 25d RR at -1.44 signals a moderate put skew as the pair tests the 160.00 psychological level where BoJ intervention risk rises. Retail sentiment is 79% short, diverging from the institutional carry bid; a break above 160.00 risks a sharp short-squeeze or official yen support.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5872 presents a tactical short-squeeze risk ahead of the RBNZ meeting on 27 May. COT LF net positioning is heavily short at -19,437 (22.3% long) while the OIS market maintains a hike bias. If the RBNZ delivers a hawkish hold or signals a hike, the crowded spec short is vulnerable to an unwind. Watch for a break above 0.5900 to confirm the squeeze.
